== Agent Rules

Rules for AI agents that can modify files, run commands, or create commits in this repository.

=== Scope boundaries

* Stay within the workspace repositories unless the user explicitly approves something broader
* `docs/cold_storage/` is archive-only context — load only when the task explicitly requires history or archaeology
* Do not modify scratch state, environment files, or generated artifacts unless the task says so
* Treat `.venv/`, `build/`, `tmp/`, `input.json`, `out.adoc`, and similar generated files as non-source

=== Actions that require explicit user confirmation

* `git push --force`, `git reset --hard`, branch deletion, or any history rewrite on a shared branch
* Publishing releases, pushing tags, or changing CI/CD behavior
* Deleting files that may represent in-progress user work
* Changing public interfaces, documented config keys, or consumer-facing import paths (see also `interfaces_and_gates.adoc`)
* Touching secrets, credentials, or infrastructure outside the repository

When in doubt: stop and ask first.

=== Working style

* Small, reviewable commits over large anonymous batches
* Explicit control flow over nested conditionals; named intermediates when values carry business or rendering semantics
* Characterization tests before reshaping dense existing logic
* Keep code, tests, backlog notes, and review notes aligned when closing an FTR
* If a task is deliberately left in-between: record the state in the backlog or a review note, not in chat history

=== Canonical sources (always load fresh)

At session start for any editing or committing task, treat the following as authoritative:

* All files in `docs/ai_info/`
* The active FTR document for the current task
* Relevant ADR(s) under `docs/internal_guide/architecture_decision_records/`
* Nearby architecture docs for the area being changed (`docs/internal_guide/architecture/`)
